feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Logo in Build Process Automation Approval Form.
entry.content:
Hi, 

We are currently working on the Build Process Automation wherein we have designed a form but unfortunately we are unable to add Logo as we don't see any option to add one. 

Can someone help us with this?

Thanks,
Gaurav

entry.title: Call Action Project error queryA ENOTFOUND in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Destination configuration 

we configured the S4HANA On-premise URL as a Destination like in a below screenshot.

Action Project

Process Automation Flow

We added the above Action project as a Dependency to our process automation flow like in a below screenshot.

Problem we are facing

Below is the response of the action activity 'GET_MAIL_LIST'.

I saw the similar issue in the below community link : Call web service error queryA ENOTFOUND - SAP Community However it didn't help us to resolve the issue.

Kindly help me to figure out the issue.

SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Build Process Automation 

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

Sakthi.

entry.title: BPA SAP Desktop Agent v3.39.45. The job has been refused error.
entry.content:
Since the v3.39.45 update SAP Desktop Agent, I'm getting this error in Desktop agent.

The Job has been refused

This is happening for all my BPA projects, when launching a process or running the automation directly in design mode. Even a very simple one-step test project with a single dialog causes the error now.

So far tried the following:

  • Downgraded both the projects and Desktop Agent to the previous, working version. (3.38.59).
  • Removing and re-registering the agent in SAP Build
  • Uninstalling and re-installing Desktop Agent from scratch.

SAP Build Process Automation 

Any suggestions because at the moment we cannot run an BPA projects.

entry.title: Build Process Automation API Trigger, Can't Get Definition ID
entry.content:
I've created a SAP Build Process Automation Process (i.e. a Workflow), with a Trigger of type 'API Call'.  I need to figure out how to call it.  I can't find SAP tutorials about exactly this, but I've pieced together some stuff from various blogs etc. including some 'official SAP blogs'.  They all have a crucial step to find the Definition ID in the 'Monitor->Manage' section (the UIs in the blogs might look slightly different than my UI, but I believe that's due to version differences).  All the blogs show the Definition ID being in a section 'Processes and Workflows' (screenshot below from one of these blogs).  However, when I go to 'Monitoring' (in my version of BPA, this shows sections and tiles, screenshot below), under the 'Manage' section I don't have a 'Processes And Workflows' tile.

If I'm going to the wrong place, I'd appreciate that info.  If the reason the tile isn't showing up is because I missed some step or some role or permission assignment or something like that, I'd appreciate that info.  Really any help at all would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: How to create an event in SAP Build Automation to call Rest IFLOW in Cloud Integration
entry.content: Hello!   We have a project that is being created in SAP Build Automation that will read an email, transform the attachment, and make a REST call to a SAP Cloud Integration IFLOW with a .json body. The Destination seems to be working as we can pull in the list of IFLOW's available.  But we're struggling on how to put the remaining pieces together to complete the process flow.   All of the blogs that I've found to date appear to be from earlier versions as their links are no longer found.

entry.title: Not able to get the workflow instance tasks in my BPA Inbox
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have come across the requirement that, we need to design approval processes that will get triggered from your ABAP env(RAP application). Based on the details, the approver will approve or reject the form.

Above is the basic travel application, the user will create the travel and send it for the approval process. As instance gets created the status for the travel is set to W(Waiting), and the approval form gets triggered.
I'm able to see the task instance in the monitor tab in the BAP but not able to see the task in the inbox of my BPA.

Monitor(Has the triggered task)

Inbox: blank

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Error importing project from another BTP sub-account.
entry.content:
I'm trying to export a project from the SAP Build Lobby of one BTP sub-account and imported into another.

I've exported / imported the required destinations and actions fine, and deployed the actions, but on importing the BPA project, I get the following error. 

Import failed.

You cannot import this external package because editable version(s) of dependencies already exists.

Correlation Id: b2a8e590-dab8-42eb-6e65-a4a2e8c41c33

Any idea what is causing this issue, or even how to track down more details on the error?

entry.title: SAP Buld Process Automation. Error: "Subscription Failed . "
entry.content:
I have a trial plan.

Subaccount: Azure/Singapor

CF is created.

Subscription Failed 

Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

entry.title: Can SAP BPA identify and input the values for the elements present in the SharePoint Portal?
entry.content:
We are facing a n issue in entering the values for the fields in SharePoint Portal. Is there any package or libraries that needs to be added for this operation?

SAP BPA is able to identify the fields but not able to enter the values and also not throwing any error.

Please suggest.

entry.title: SAP GUI option is not showing in the Technology drop-down in SAP Build Process Automation for S4HANA
entry.content:
Hello experts, I would like to need support on the query below:

I am exploring the recorder function in the SAP Build Process Automation, and all the pre-requisities have been completed.

  • Access to a SAP BTP tenant with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Install and set up the Desktop Agent 3 to run the automation
  • Scripting enabled for SAP GUI for Windows on the Client and the Server
  • Only use 32-bit SAP GUI for Windows Application
  • SAP GUI for Windows must run in the background

The issue that I have noticed is that SAP GUI Technology is not showing in the dropdown menu if you are using the S4HANA version.

The below screenshot was taken for SAP ECC, and here SAP GUI is showing in the Technology dropdown. And the business process recording works perfrectly fine.

 

The below screenshot was taken for the SAP S/4HANA 2023 version. You can see no SAP GUI option is showing in the Technology dropdown in the SAP Build Process Automation Recorder option/feature.

 

Furthermore, if you press the record button and start the recording, then the recorder throws an error message that "The screen could not be captured. Please relaunch the recorder."

Below is the RZ11 screenshot:

Could you guys please provide the valuable and appropriate solution to resolve the issue. Your response would be much appreciated.

Regards,

Mubasher

entry.title: How to fetch known error in Action call for SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we have created a process within SAP Build Process automation. The process includes an action call after an approval task.

Within the CAP application which is handling the action call we have defined known errors that will be returned as follows:

return req.error({
    code: 123, // internal code for identifying return messages
    message: "my return message",
    target: "where is the message caused",
    status: 4## //the error status
})

An error could be that a specific action was not done before approval by the approver himself.

However, if the action does not retrieve a success return (HTTP return status equals 4##) the whole process is put to an error state. Is there any possibility to catch this error within the process itself and send a notification to the MyInbox of a specific group/user?

Actually, we don't want the process to get into an error state for this scenario but instead execute an alternative path within the process itself.

 

regards

Emanuel

entry.title: How to Dynamically Set Values in UI Elements Using a Loop in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Dear All,

  • I would appreciate your help in understanding how to dynamically set values for multiple input fields within a loop.

  • Scenario: I have several input fields that follow an incremental naming pattern.

  • InputField1

  • InputField2

  • InputField3

  • InputField4

  • I want to loop through these fields and assign values to them dynamically.

  •  

Thank you for your assistance!

Details- I want to automate a website to extract the PO Number and input the corresponding details such as Ship Date, Order Total, Agent Name, and more. I need to do this repeatedly within a single loop. While other RPA tools can accomplish this, I am having trouble with SAP BPA. I would appreciate any guidance, best practices, or example implementations for managing dynamic UI elements in SAP BPA.

 

Page link-(https://pathfinder.automationanywhere.com/challenges/automationanywherelabs-supplychainmanagement.html?)

entry.title: Subscription failed for sap build process automation
entry.content:
Team,

This happened while I was working in - trail BTP cockpit - Singapore Region -subaccount

Attached is the screenshot

CF info
https://api.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com 

PS: Referred to previous similar posts with same issue, but that did not help

entry.title: Need Help Creating An API Call In SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I'm having trouble figuring out how to call an API from my Process/Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.  I've scanned some questions/posts on this site, and I currently have these 'targeted questions' whose answers would help refine my understanding and might lead me to further questions.  Any help that could be given would be greatly appreciated.

IMPORTANT BACKGROUND: My organization has some administrative constraints around new subscriptions to things like SAP Build Process Automation.  So I'm 'learning and building' the Process/Workflow (that we'll ultimately need to deploy to the organization's BTP) in my own trial BTP account.  My organization's BTP has been in use (without any subscription to BPA) for a while and is being used, etc.  It has a Destination where the CAP/CDS project or whatever that would serve this OData endpoint, is 'hosted'.  Endpoints deployed into that Destination are reachable/callable from 'anywhere', like Postman running on my local machine.  In the Business Process in my trial BTP account, I was thinking I could call over to that, but I'm beginning to think that I can only call to Destinations configured in the same 'BTP space' or whatever, as the Process.

QUESTIONS I HAVE

Is the best way for me to approach this in my Process/Workflow - is the best approach to have a Destination and then be using that Destination? (once I'm building this in my organization's BTP 'space', this Destination would already be there though it looks like I might need to add some attributes etc. to it.  While I'm working a trial account, I'd just set up a Destination with a 'passthrough endpoint' whose implementation just uses executeHttpRequest etc., to 'call over' to the endpoint in my organization's BTP Destination, etc.).

Once I've added and correctly added an configuration, is it true that then in my Process/Workflow, I should be able to add an Action and my endpoint (or somehow the Destination with some sort of 'drill-down' to the endpoint, etc.) would then show up in the Action box?

Any help would be greatly appreciated.  Thanks!

entry.title: How to attach the uploaded files of sap BPA to email
entry.content:
Hi all,

In sap build process automation workflow, I used DMS to upload files.

Also, I used email notification for user alert.

And, I would like to attach the uploaded file from BPA workflow to email that can be able to download the files from users.

Is it possible to do so?

If yes, how can I develop that kind of setting?

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: SAP Process Automation - Desktop Agent Disconnects When Signing Out of Server
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created an automation in SAP Build Process Automation for a client and installed the SAP Desktop Agent on the client’s server. The client’s server runs continuously, as I also have a Cloud Connector installed on the same server, which operates all the time. During registration, I used my BTP user ID to register the agent and also made sure the agent service is installed. The agent is running continuously and executing jobs successfully.

However, I am facing an issue:

  • The automation works perfectly when I am logged into the server.

  • But when I sign out of the server, the agent disconnects, and the jobs stop running.

To resolve this, I have already:

  • Configured the "Open and Unlock Session Automatically" option with the server name and credentials.

  • Ensured that the Desktop Agent service is running in "unattended" mode.

Unfortunately, this did not work, and the agent still disconnects when I sign out of the server.

My Questions:

  1. How can I keep the SAP Desktop Agent running even after signing out of the server?

  2. Are there any additional server or agent configurations needed to ensure automation continues running in the background?

  3. Should I use a service account instead of a personal BTP user ID for agent registration?

Any guidance on this would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

@SAPSupport 
@maheshpalavalli 
@Archana 
@Dan_Wroblewski 
@Sankara1 
@Venkat_Vyza

entry.title: Gmail Integration with SAP Build Process Automation (RPA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a custom automation in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) using the Gmail features from the Google Workspace SDK. My goal is to automate data extraction from Gsheet file attached in Gmail. However, I ran into an issue right at the beginning, when trying to search for the email, the automation fails with the following error:

Error: Please specify user email Id
Exception: irpa_core.error.SequenceError

#Configuration

  • I'm using a Google service account key for authentication.

  • I call the "Google Authorization" activity at the start of the automation. I provided the userEmail generated in the Service Account key, and the authorization is successfully executed without any issue.

  • Search Email query parameter : "subject:" + Step0.searchSubject + " AND from:" + Step0.searchFrom.

  • Domain-wide delegation is configured in Google Admin Console :

   - https://mail.google.com
   - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive
   - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify
   - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform

Has anyone experienced this? Could there be an internal requirement to pass the userEmail again within the Gmail task itself?

Any help, guidance, or shared experience would be greatly appreciated 🙏
Thanks in advance!
Kais

entry.title: Getting error while installing Desktop Agent 3
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am getting below error while installing Desktop agent 3. May I know what need to be done to resolve this issue?

I am following below link to install desktop agent

Install and Set Up Desktop Agent 3 | SAP Tutorials

 

 

 

 

Regards,

Jagadhatri

 

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Not able to call a s/4hana(on premise) api using action projects from sap build process automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are trying to call s/4hana standard api for business partner using action projects.

Below is the partial url -

"sap/opu/odata/sap/md_businesspartner_srv/C_BPEmailAddress" that is setup in the destination.

While executing our automation code, it fails with  below errors-

error messages-

Action execution resulted in ECONNRESET with message read ECONNRESET

query ENOFOUND <<our hostname>>

note- our desktop agent version - 3.39.45

We have setup cloud connector setting as per guide and destination is also working fine.

The url works fine at browser level as well.

Has anybody noticed any similar errors recently?

This setup was working fine before but has now stopped working suddenly from jan 2025 onwards.

Please advise.

Regards,

Saritha

entry.title: Missing steps in "Create and Edit the Automation" Tutorial
entry.content:
I am following the Learning Journey "Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation" (https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/create-processes-and-automations-with-sap-build-process-automation )

Section : Boosting the Sales Order Management Process with an Automation

Exercise: Create and Edit the Automation

Pg. 17, Step 52 of pdf: chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://learnsap.enable-now.cloud.sap/pub/mmcp/project/PR_D1366796546A5FA0/hands_on.pdf

Choose Sales Order > I cannot choose Sales Order even though I have created the Data Type as described in the Tutorial. The new data type is not available as a possible selection.

Data Type exists:

Not available for Selecting as Output Parameter:

I watched the video but it skips this part as well (2:41 of video)
